name: Duke Pearson
abstract: Columbus Calvin "Duke" Pearson Jr. (August 17, 1932 – August 4, 1980) was an American jazz pianist and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: Duke Pearson is a friend of mine, he’s been dead fifteen years
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mickey Roker
about_quotes.evidence: Duke Pearson more or less taught me how to read you know. Because he had a big band. And then he asked me to join his big band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mickey Roker interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I think — what was the guy’s name — Duke Pearson had a big band and there was always some kind of workshop band that had the fine musicians that were doing studio and stuff like that and wanted to play jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Duke Pearson arranged for the album 'A New Perspective' which Kenny Burrell played on, but Kenny Burrell does not mention Duke Pearson in his response or elsewhere in the transcript.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, for Donald, for Sonny Red, for Duke Pearson, for Herbie Hancock. I look back at my stuff and I see that I copied and arranged it and got paid $10
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: I mean Duke Pearson’s band rehearsed every week for a year and they never called him a “rehearsal band.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
